statement: The March 2026 Gallup/Vísir survey on the EU referendum showed sharp party-based divisions: Viðreisn voters are most supportive of continuing negotiations, followed by Samfylkingin voters. Miðflokkurinn and Sjálfstæðisflokkurinn voters are most opposed. Framsóknarflokkurinn voters are roughly evenly split, with approximately half opposed. Among those who support the current government, 83% back continuing EU negotiations. The party divide mirrors the coalition/opposition split: the two explicitly pro-EU coalition parties (Samfylkingin, Viðreisn) have strongly pro-negotiation voter bases, while the opposition parties that have historically opposed EU membership retain EU-sceptic electorates.
source_name: Gallup Ísland / Vísir — EU referendum survey, March 2026
caveats: Party subsamples are small (especially for smaller parties), so individual party breakdowns have wide margins of error. The survey measures support for 'continuing negotiations' rather than EU membership per se. Flokkur fólksins, which is in the coalition but EU-sceptic, may have split voters — this is not detailed in the published results.

related_entries.statement: The Gallup Þjóðarpúls survey conducted February–March 2026 (sample size approximately 9,958, participation rate 43.3%) showed 42% of Icelanders support EU membership and 42% oppose it, with approximately 16% undecided. This represents a statistical dead heat, consistent with the trend of gradually rising support from ~35% in 2022 to ~45% in late 2025, now stabilising. A separate question on the upcoming referendum found 52% favour continuing EU accession negotiations versus 48% opposed — a higher figure than membership support, reflecting voters who want negotiations to proceed without necessarily supporting the end result of membership.

The March 2026 Gallup/Vísir survey on the EU referendum showed a significant gender gap: 62% of women support continuing EU accession negotiations versus 22% opposed, while 52% of men support continuing versus 39% opposed. Women are thus considerably more pro-negotiation than men. This gender gap is consistent with historical patterns in Icelandic EU polling, where women have consistently been more favourable toward EU membership by 10–15 percentage points. The pattern may reflect different prioritisation of issues: men are more likely to cite fisheries sovereignty concerns, while women are more likely to prioritise economic stability and social policy alignment.

The Althingi (Icelandic parliament) has 63 seats. As of session 157, 57 seats are held by sitting MPs. The party breakdown is: Sjálfstæðisflokkur (Independence Party) 13, Samfylkingin (Social Democrats) 13, Viðreisn (Reform Party) 11, Miðflokkurinn (Centre Party) 8, Flokkur fólksins (People's Party) 8, Framsóknarflokkur (Progressive Party) 4. The remaining 6 seats are vacant or held by alternates (varamenn).

citations.report_title: Þjóðin á báðum áttum um viðræður: 52% gegn 48%

claims.claim_text: Kjósendur Viðreisnar og Samfýlkingarinnar eru helst hlynntir þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslu um framhald aðildarviðræðna.
Kjósendur Flokks fólksins eru einnig hlynntir þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslu um framhald aðildarviðræðna.
Kjósendur Miðflokksins og Sjálfstæðisflokksins eru helst andvígir þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslu.
Kjósendur Viðreisnar og Samfýlkingarinnar telja helst að þau myndu kjósa með framhaldi aðildarviðræðna fremur en kjósendur stjórnarandstöðuflokkanna.
Kjósendur Viðreisnar eru hvað hlynntastir þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslunni.
Kjósendur Miðflokksins og Sjálfstæðisflokksins eru andvígastir þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslunni.
Rúmur helmingur kjósenda Framsóknarflokksins er andvígur þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslunni.
83 prósent þeirra sem styðja ríkisstjórnina eru hlynntir þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslunni.
Marktækur munur er á afstöðu til þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslunnar eftir afstöðu til ríkisstjórnarinnar.

citations.report_title: „Við hefðum náð meiri árangri með því að vera innan Evrópusambandsins“

claims.claim_text: Formenn ríkisstjórnarflokkanna greindu frá því á föstudag 7. mars 2026 að til standi að halda þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslu um áframhaldandi aðildarviðræður við ESB.
